utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: stephanie morales , medical record number 113322 . 44-year-old female that presents today six weeks status post right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello !

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how's the knee treating you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . any problems ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no problems , uh , just still the numbness on this side .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . that area of numbness will get smaller as the nerves that innervate the skin across the knee heal .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right . okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so lem me take a look at your right knee .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , and relax for me . good . all right , and bend . good . that looks good . um , the incision looks nice .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's look at your x-rays . uh , they look good . so here's what you got going on in there . uh , it looks good . everything's lined up nicely . um , that's looking at it from the front . and this is looking at it from the side . and then this was underneath your kneecap , but we did n't do anything there . but things looks great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i'm so happy with how the surgery went . i have no pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , you know , you're still early in your recovery , so i expect you to get even better than what you are now . um , so keep doing your pt to work on your strength , range of motion , and endurance .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , will do .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're probably still gon na have aches and pains occasionally . i usually tell people it's two or three months before you feel like you're back to yourself again , so you're doing really well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . that's really good to know . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , let's plan to talk again in six weeks . so if you stop at the desk on your way out today , they're going to get you set up for a telemedicine visit . and that visit'll just be to check in and see how you're doing and make sure things are progressing okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure , okay . and can i ask you about work before i go ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , as you know , i work for ups , and i'm not able to go back yet because of going up and down steps with the position i have . however , they're asking me to come back to work where i can sit for the shift with breaks .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i , i do n't want you lifting greater than five or 10 pounds , and no standing or sitting longer than a half an hour . uh , usually that means you ca n't do a ups job .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . so would you write a note to excuse me for another six weeks ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure . i'll have my nurse anna give it to you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's great . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so we'll talk again in six weeks , and we can discuss your job again at that time .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , those , um , are the types of , of jobs that take a good three months until you're kind of ready to get back to doing that kind of labor-intensive work .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , and driving now ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , we have plenty of data that says normal reflex time is returned at six weeks , and then your return to driving is determined by when you're ready and feel safe to be driving .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah . so hang tight . anna will bring that note back in to you , and we'll talk in six weeks .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , but keep working hard on , on pt in the meantime though .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i definitely will . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , have a good rest of your day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you as well .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , this is a six-week post-op meeting exam for her . her gait was antalgic with a slight limp and decreased velocity . she's not using an assistive device . right knee focused exam has a healed surgical scar , mild swelling , tenderness- tenderness .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . on the area of the incision . range of motion is zero to 120 degrees . everything else normal . radiographs were three views of the right knee taken today and show implants in stable position .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no evidence of loosening or early complications . thanks .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 44-year-old female presenting for a six-week follow-up after a right medial unicompartmental knee arthroplasty.
Knee recovery status.
The doctor asks how the patient's knee is doing.
The doctor asks if the patient is experiencing any problems.
The patient reports no pain but notes persistent numbness on the lateral aspect of the knee.
The doctor explains that the numbness is normal and will decrease as the nerves heal.
Physical examination.
The doctor performs a physical exam of the right knee, noting a healed incision.
Radiograph review.
The doctor reviews the X-rays, noting the implants are stable and properly aligned.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue physical therapy for strength, range of motion, and endurance.
The doctor notes that occasional aches are normal and full recovery typically takes two to three months.
The doctor schedules a follow-up telemedicine visit in six weeks.
Return to work.
The patient asks about returning to work.
The patient explains her job at UPS requires physical activity and she is currently unable to return.
The doctor sets work restrictions of no lifting over 5-10 pounds and no standing or sitting longer than 30 minutes.
The patient asks for a work excuse note for another six weeks.
The doctor agrees to provide a work excuse note.
The doctor states that labor-intensive work typically requires three months for recovery.
Driving.
The patient asks when she can resume driving.
The doctor advises that driving can resume when the patient feels safe, noting normal reflex time returns by six weeks.
The doctor documents an antalgic gait with a slight limp, no assistive device, and a healed surgical scar with mild swelling.
The doctor documents range of motion as 0 to 120 degrees and stable implants on radiographs.

case_description: postoperative right medial knee unicompartmental arthroplasty follow up; none
